name: m_conversation_multi_request_integration_ask
notes: Long multi-request single message, variation B: bundled around the integration/readiness beat instead of win-back delegation — an affirmation + a data-privacy question + a platform-support question, all in ONE message. Distinct from variation A (status/new_task/pricing) and from multi_field_single_message.json (profile FIELDS, not distinct request kinds). VT-465 gap: Instagram is not a wired connector today, only Shopify — the reply must not fabricate an Instagram connection. Setup: `setup --onboarded --flow ready_asked --name convo-harness-<id>` then `script <tenant> m_conversation_multi_request_integration_ask.json`.

steps.message: Yes let's connect — also while we're at it, what happens to my data once it's connected, and by the way do you support Instagram shop too or only Shopify?
steps.note: Three asks in one message: readiness affirm + a privacy question + a platform-support question. Expect the store connection to be engaged, a real data-handling answer, and an honest capability answer on Instagram (not wired) — never a fabricated Instagram connection.

steps.message: ok so just to be clear, which one should I go with
steps.note: Follow-up referencing the platform question from turn 1. Expect a coherent recommendation (Shopify, the wired connector) — not a re-ask of which platform the owner uses.
